blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلْعَصْرِ (۱)
By Time! (1)
إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ لَفِى خُسْرٍ (۲)
Man is indeed in loss, (2)
إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ (۳)
except those who have faith and do righteous deeds, and enjoin one another to [follow] the truth, and enjoin one another to patience. (3)

Key Points

In the early days of Islam, Muslims used to recite this Surah when bidding farewell and separating from one another. (144)

Allah has sworn by all segments of time in the Quran. Dawn (Fajr): «والفجر» (145), Morning (Subh): «و الصبح» (146), Day (Nahar): «و النّهار» (147), Night (Layl): «و الّیل» (148), Forenoon (Duha): «و الضُحى » (149), Predawn (Sahar): «والّیل اذ ادبر» (150), Afternoon (Asr): «و العصر», although He has sworn by the predawn several times.

In a Hadith narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, it is stated that the intent is the era of the emergence of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام. (151) Some have taken the meaning of Asr to be the era of the rise of Islam. Some have taken Asr in its literal meaning, which is pressure, because pressures lead to the removal of heedlessness, and encourage human effort and innovation. Some have taken the intent of Asr to be the essence of existence, meaning the Perfect Man, and some have considered it to be the time of the Asr prayer. (152)

Loss of wealth is compensable, but the loss of humanity is the greatest of losses. «ان الخاسرین الّذین خسروا انفسهم» (153)

Advising others to the truth (Haqq) may have bitter consequences, which must be met with patience and steadfastness. «تواصوا بالحقّ و تواصوا بالصبر»

Fakhr Razi says: Man in this world is like an ice seller whose capital melts away every moment, and he must sell it as quickly as possible, otherwise he will suffer loss. (154)

Although advising to truth and patience are part of righteous deeds (A'mal Salih), they have been mentioned separately due to their importance. Similarly, patience and steadfastness are also truth, and «تواصوا بالحقّ» includes them, but patience is mentioned separately due to its significance. «تواصوا بالحقّ و تواصوا بالصّبر»

In another Ayah, the Quran considers Divine grace and mercy to be the factor preventing loss, stating: «فلو لا فضل اللّه علیكم و رحمته لكنتم من الخاسرین» (155) Therefore, faith, righteous deeds, and advising to the truth are achieved under the shade of Divine grace and mercy, and without the favor of Allah, man is neither guided to attain faith nor becomes capable of righteous deeds.

The World Market A brief look at the verses of the Quran leads us to this fundamental point: the world is a market, and all people offer their lifespan, abilities, and talents in it, and the sale of goods in this market is compulsory. Hadrat Ali عليه السلام says: «نفس المرء خطاه الى اجله» (156), meaning that a person's breathing is a step towards his death. Thus, man inevitably loses the capital of his life every moment, and we cannot prevent the passing of our life and ability. Whether we want it or not, our ability and time slip away from us. Therefore, what is important in this market is the choice of the customer. Some individuals trade with Allah, placing everything they possess on the platter of sincerity, and taking steps in His path and for His pleasure. These individuals make their fleeting life eternal and trade the world for Paradise and Divine pleasure. This group consists of the true winners, because, firstly, their Buyer is Allah, to Whom all existence belongs. Secondly, He buys even insignificant goods. «فمن یعمل مثقال ذرّة خیراً یره» (157) Thirdly, He buys at a high price, and His reward is eternal Paradise. «خالدین فیها» (158) Fourthly, if we strive to do good deeds but are not successful in performing them, He still rewards us. «لیس للانسان الا ما سعى» (159), meaning that if someone strives, even if the action is not completed, he succeeds. Fifthly, He gives the reward manifold. «اضعافاً مضاعفة» (160) According to the Quran, He rewards up to seven hundred times, and the reward for good spending (infaq) is like a grain from which seven ears grow, and in every ear there are a hundred grains.

However, those who sell their lives in this market to their own desires or the desires of others, and do not think of the pleasure of Allah, have suffered a heavy loss, regarding which the Quran uses such expressions: They have made a bad trade. «بئسما اشتروا به انفسهم» (161) Their trade yields no profit. «فما ربحت تجارتهم» (162) They have lost. «خسروا انفسهم» (163) They have suffered a manifest loss. «خسراناً مبیناً» (164) They are immersed in loss. «لفى خسر» (165)

In the view of this group, the clever and shrewd person is one who can create a comfortable life for himself through any action, word, or method, whether right or wrong, and gain fame, popularity, position, or a medal in society. Otherwise, they label him as a loser, backward, and wretched. But in Islamic culture, the shrewd person is one who holds his soul accountable and does not let it loose, works for eternal life, and whose every day is better than his yesterday. He is not heedless of death and turns to piety, contentment, and justice instead of frivolity, greed, and oppression. Imam Hadi عليه السلام said: «الدّنیا سوق ربح قوم و خسر آخرون» The world is a market where a group profited and another group lost. (166)

144) Tafasir Namuneh and Durr al-Manthur. 145) Fajr, 1. 146) Takwir, 18. 147) Layl, 2. 148) Layl, 1. 149) Duha, 1. 150) Muddaththir, 33. 151) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 152) Tafsir Namuneh. 153) Zumar, 15. 154) Tafsir Kabir. 155) Baqarah, 64. 156) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 74. 157) Zilzal, 7. 158) Aal Imran, 15. 159) Najm, 39. 160) Aal Imran, 130. 161) Baqarah, 90. 162) Baqarah, 16. 163) A'raf, 53. 164) Nisa, 119. 165) Asr, 2. 166) Tuhaf al-Uqul, Sayings of Imam Hadi.

Messages

1- The span of human history has value, and Allah has sworn by it. Therefore, let us take heed from its lessons. «والعصر»

2- Man is in loss from every direction. «لفى خسر»

3- Absolute man, who is not within the orbit of the Prophets' training, is in a state of loss. «انّ الانسان لفى خسر»

4- The only way to prevent loss is faith and action. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

5- Thinking only of oneself is not enough. The believer thinks about the growth and elevation of others. «تواصوا بالحقّ»

6- Advising to patience is just as necessary as advising to the truth. «بالحقّ - بالصبر»

7- Faith precedes action, just as self-building precedes community-building. «آمنوا و عملوا... و تواصوا»

8- Without faith, righteous deeds, and advising others to truth and patience, human loss is very great. «لفى خسر» (The indefiniteness of khusr and its tanwin (nunation) is a sign of the magnitude of the loss.)

9- Establishing the truth requires steadfastness. «تواصوا بالحق و تواصوا بالصبر»

10- Society is reformed when all people participate in enjoining good and forbidding evil. They should both advise and accept advice. «تواصوا بالحقّ» (The word tawasaw is for reciprocal action.)

11- Salvation from loss occurs when man intends to perform all good deeds, even if he does not succeed in completing them. «عملوا الصالحات» (The word al-Salihat is used in the definite plural form with alif and lam.)

12- Faith must be comprehensive, not partial. Faith in all parts of the religion, not just some of it. «الاّ الّذین آمنوا» (Faith is mentioned absolutely so that it includes all sacred matters.)
